evals.prompt: Analyze my portfolio: AAPL:30 MSFT:20 NVDA:50 — is my risk well-balanced?
evals.expected_output: Identifies NVDA as risk-dominant position (~66% vol contribution for 50% weight), flags 100% tech concentration, compares to SPY benchmark, and suggests diversification improvements

assertions.name: identifies_risk_dominant_position
assertions.text: Points out NVDA contributes disproportionate risk relative to its weight

assertions.name: explains_correlation_impact
assertions.text: Discusses how correlations between holdings affect portfolio risk

assertions.name: benchmark_comparison
assertions.text: Compares portfolio metrics (return, vol, Sharpe, drawdown) against SPY

assertions.name: flags_sector_concentration
assertions.text: Notes 100% Technology sector exposure as a risk

assertions.name: recommends_next_steps
assertions.text: Suggests specific actions — diversify into other sectors, trim risk-dominant position, or use other skills for deeper analysis

evals.prompt: I'm building a diversified portfolio: AAPL:25 MSFT:25 JPM:25 XOM:25 — how well-diversified is it really?
evals.expected_output: Acknowledges multi-sector exposure (Tech 50%, Financials 25%, Energy 25%), highlights low MSFT-XOM correlation as genuine diversification, notes risk contributions are more balanced than the concentrated portfolio

assertions.name: sector_exposure_analysis
assertions.text: Breaks down sector weights and comments on diversification quality

assertions.name: correlation_interpretation
assertions.text: Interprets specific correlation pairs — highlights low correlations as diversification benefit

assertions.name: risk_balance_assessment
assertions.text: Evaluates whether risk contributions are proportional to weights

assertions.name: benchmark_relative_performance
assertions.text: Compares Sharpe ratio and drawdown against SPY

assertions.name: honest_assessment
assertions.text: Notes that while more diversified than a pure tech portfolio, 4 stocks and 50% tech still has concentration risk

evals.prompt: Check my HK portfolio: 0700.HK:40 9988.HK:30 1810.HK:30 — how is it doing vs the Hang Seng?
evals.expected_output: Compares against 2800.HK benchmark, discusses HK market context, flags high Tencent-Alibaba correlation, notes underperformance vs benchmark

assertions.name: uses_hk_benchmark
assertions.text: Compares against 2800.HK (Hang Seng Tracker) not SPY

assertions.name: discusses_underperformance
assertions.text: Notes portfolio underperformance vs benchmark and explains why (concentration in specific names vs broad index)

assertions.name: high_correlation_warning
assertions.text: Flags the high 0700.HK-9988.HK correlation as reducing diversification benefit

assertions.name: drawdown_context
assertions.text: Contextualizes max drawdown in absolute terms and relative to benchmark

assertions.name: actionable_for_hk_investor
assertions.text: Provides HK-market-relevant suggestions, not generic US-centric advice

evals.prompt: Analyze my portfolio: AAPL:10 MSFT:10 GOOGL:10 AMZN:10 META:10 JPM:10 V:10 UNH:10 XOM:10 PG:10 — is this well-balanced?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes 10-stock equal-weight portfolio across multiple sectors, evaluates sector diversification, notes which correlations provide real diversification benefit, compares to SPY

assertions.name: recognizes_equal_weight
assertions.text: Notes the equal-weight structure and its implications for rebalancing

assertions.name: multi_sector_assessment
assertions.text: Evaluates the sector distribution across tech, financials, healthcare, energy, consumer staples

assertions.name: identifies_diversification_benefit
assertions.text: Points to specific low-correlation pairs that provide genuine risk reduction

assertions.name: portfolio_vs_index
assertions.text: Compares portfolio construction to the benchmark — is this a closet indexer or meaningfully different?

assertions.name: risk_decomposition_insight
assertions.text: Identifies which holdings contribute most/least to portfolio risk despite equal weights
